div.div:
Is ≥ 50% visible AND
block bottom inside
the scroll area?

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 358px; height: 1px; padding-top: 430px; margin-left: 62px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0px; text-align: center;

div.div:
YES → block eligible;
dwell accumulates via rAF when scroll is slow

switch.text: YES → block eligible; dwell via rAF...

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 358px; height: 1px; padding-top: 480px; margin-left: 62px;

div.div:
NO → discard partial dwell
(scrolled past or not yet seen)

div.div:
Is scroll speed
≤ WPM-scaled cap?
(1200 px/s at 200 WPM)
batch samples ≥ 50 ms
velocity = |ΣΔy| / ΣΔt

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 358px; height: 1px; padding-top: 430px; margin-left: 742px;

div.div:
YES → dwell ms added each frame
(until block threshold reached)

switch.text: YES → dwell ms added each frame...

NO → pause dwell + clear accumulation
(user is flinging / skimming)

All gates OK

div.div:
• Fast fling (velocity > WPM-scaled cap)
• Block leaves view before dwell completes
• Idle 45 s → dwell paused, partial cleared

• rAF loop adds ms until block threshold
• Block added to confirmed set → % saved
